data.title: The USD is losing some ground in Asian trade
data.description:
The USD is slipping vs. other major FX in Asia trade.

Apart from what has been posted there isn't any fresh news.

EUR, AUD, CAD, GBP, NZD all higher. USD/JPY and USD/CHF are losing a few tics also.

EUR/USD:

data.title: Mirxes Shares Jump 24% on Hong Kong Debut
data.description:
Mirxes made an impressive trading debut in Hong Kong on Friday morning as its shares soared over 24% at the opening bell.

The Singapore-headquartered micro ribonucleic acid technology company opened at HK$29 per share, well above its IPO price of HK$23.30.

data.title: MTAR Technologies Plans Merger with Two Wholly Owned Arms to Streamline Operations
data.description:
MTAR Technologies approved a proposal to merge with its two wholly owned subsidiaries, Gee Pee Aerospace and Defence and Magnatar Aerosystems, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

The merger is intended to simplify the group's structure, reduce administrative costs, and improve financial consolidation. No new shares will be issued under the proposed scheme.

data.title: Dhunseri Tea to Sell Deohall Tea Estate
data.description:
Dhunseri Tea & Industries agreed to sell its Deohall tea estate to Madhuting Tea for 235 million Indian rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The transaction is expected to be completed by June 30.

data.title: Replimune Group Executives and Director Sell Shares to Cover Tax Obligations
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Sarchi ChristopherChief Commercial OfficerSell$30,179Form 4
Hill Emily LuisaChief Financial OfficerSell$20,406Form 4
Patel SushilChief Executive OfficerSell$202,346Form 4
Schwendenman AndrewChief Accounting OfficerSell$26,460Form 4
Xynos KonstantinosChief Medical OfficerSell$64,093Form 4
Astley-Sparke PhilipDirectorSell$260,168Form 4

Several executives and a director at Replimune Group have sold shares of the company's common stock to cover tax withholding obligations related to the vesting of Restricted Stock Units.

Christopher Sarchi, Chief Commercial Officer, sold 3,749 shares at a weighted average price of $8.05 per share, totaling $30,179. Following the sale, Sarchi directly owns 128,296 shares.

Emily Luisa Hill, Chief Financial Officer, sold 2,535 shares at a weighted average price of $8.05 per share, totaling $20,406. Hill now directly owns 143,522 shares.

Sushil Patel, Chief Executive Officer, sold 25,105 shares at a weighted average price of $8.06 per share, totaling $202,346. Patel's direct ownership stands at 343,576 shares following the transaction.

Andrew Schwendenman, Chief Accounting Officer, sold 3,287 shares at a weighted average price of $8.05 per share, totaling $26,460. Schwendenman directly owns 68,284 shares post-sale.

Konstantinos Xynos, Chief Medical Officer, sold 7,952 shares at a weighted average price of $8.06 per share, totaling $64,093. Following the transaction, Xynos directly owns 146,933 shares.

Philip Astley-Sparke, a Director, sold 32,279 shares at a weighted average price of $8.06 per share, totaling $260,168. Astley-Sparke directly owns 1,405,071 shares after the sale.

data.title: Meituan's First-Quarter Profit Likely Jumped on Stronger Revenue — Earnings Preview
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

Meituan is scheduled to report results for the first quarter on Monday. Here is what you need to know:

NET PROFIT: The Chinese food-delivery company is expected to report net profit of 7.93 billion yuan, equivalent to $1.10 billion, according to the consensus estimate of nine analysts polled by FactSet. That would be sharply higher than the 5.37 billion yuan reported in the year-earlier period.

REVENUE: Revenue is estimated at 85.4 billion yuan, according to FactSet, up from 73.275 billion yuan a year ago.

The company's Hong Kong-listed shares rose 2.7% in the first three months of the year, underperforming the benchmark Hang Seng Index's 15% gain that came amid optimism about Chinese AI startup DeepSeek and Beijing's more favorable stance toward private enterprises. Since then, Meituan's stock has fallen about 13% as macroeconomic uncertainties weighed on investors' sentiment globally.

WHAT TO WATCH:

  • DOMESTIC COMPETITION: Investors will be interested in whether stiff competition from other Chinese food-delivery players has hindered Meituan's revenue growth. Citi analysts said in a research note that the initial food-delivery volume achieved by e-commerce giant JD.com, a new entrant, is expected to have a limited impact on Meituan's volume in the near term. However, they said the increased competition will likely force Meituan to step up platform subsidies for users, riders and merchants and accelerate the rollout of rider benefits, which will weigh on revenue growth and unit economics, an indicator measuring profitability per order in the delivery industry.
  • OVERSEAS EXPANSION: Meituan is expected to provide an update on its overseas-expansion initiatives after the results, Citi analysts wrote in a separate note. While Brazil could be the company's next focus, it might also expand its Keeta app to other Arab nations in 2025, they said. The analysts noted that Meituan's international food-delivery app could enjoy a high user penetration, frequency and average order value in these new markets.

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com

data.title: Ardelyx Executive Sells Shares to Cover Taxes
data.description:
Reporter NameFoster Eric Duane
RelationshipChief Commercial Officer
TypeSell
Amount$10,232
SEC FilingForm 4

Eric Duane Foster, Chief Commercial Officer of Ardelyx, sold 2,507 shares of Common Stock on May 20, 2025, at a price of $4.0814 per share, totaling $10,232. The sale was conducted to cover applicable withholding taxes upon the vesting of restricted stock units. Following this transaction, Foster directly owns 316,806 shares of Ardelyx. The reported sale includes an adjustment for the purchase of 2,633 shares under the Ardelyx Employee Stock Purchase Plan in February 2025.

SEC Filing: ARDELYX, INC. [ ARDX ] - Form 4 - May. 22, 2025

data.title: Adroit Infotech Says Rights Issue Proceeds Used as Planned
data.description:
Adroit Infotech (, NSE:ADROITPP) said there was no deviation or variation in the use of proceeds from its 457.2 million-rupee rights issue completed on Feb. 14, according to a statement submitted to Indian stock exchanges.

The funds were allocated toward business expansion, repayment of non-current liabilities, working capital needs, general corporate purposes, and issue-related expenses, the company said.

A portion earmarked for acquisitions remains unutilized and is held in an account with Axis Bank.

data.title: Philippine cbank says it has room for two more rate cuts in 2025
data.description:
There is room to cut Philippine interest rates by 25 basis points twice more this year, but they will not necessarily be at consecutive meetings and the central bank has to be careful not to cut by too much, Governor Eli Remolona said on Friday.

Inflation should remain low in coming months, with growth expected to moderate this year and next, he told a briefing, adding he expected the current economic uncertainty to persist.

"We still have to be careful because we don't want to cut too much," Remolona told reporters.

The central bank resumed its easing cycle last month, cutting its key policy rate (PHCBIR=ECI) by 25 basis points to 5.5%. The next policy meeting is scheduled for June 19.

Annual inflation slowed to 1.4% in April, the lowest rate since November 2019. This brought the year-to-date average inflation to 2.0%, the bottom of the central bank's 2.0% to 4.0% target range for the year.

data.title: Silk Logistics Holdings Says ACCC Resumes Review of Proposed Acquisition by DP World Australia
data.description:
Silk Logistics Holdings said that the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission resumed its review regarding the proposed acquisition of all of the shares in the firm by DP World Australia via a scheme of arrangement, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The revised date for announcement of the regulator's findings is July 10. The scheme meeting is currently schedule for June 20. The end date under the scheme implementation deed is June 30.

The firm is reviewing its position regarding the revised ACCC decision date and is in talks with DP World Australia.

Silk Logistics said that it has not issued a notice to extend the end date under the scheme and it has not received such a notice from DP World.

data.title: Luharuka Media Renamed DhanSafal Finserve to Reflect Financial Services Focus
data.description:
Luharuka Media & Infra Ltd has rebranded as DhanSafal Finserve Ltd, marking its shift toward financial services, the company said in a local filing on Thursday.

DhanSafal will roll out its new identity across platforms in the coming weeks, including a revised stock ticker.

data.title: Indus Towers to Buy 26% Stake in JSW Green Energy Eight SPV for INR380 Million
data.description:
Indus Towers will acquire a 26% stake in JSW Green Energy Eight, a special purpose vehicle for solar power, for about 380.4 million Indian rupees in cash, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

The telecom infrastructure company said the move supports its renewable energy procurement under a captive model and aligns with its net zero goals.

data.title: Channel Infrastructure Sees Higher 2025 Dividend; Shares Near 6.5-Year High
data.description:
Channel Infrastructure NZ expects 2025 dividend in the lower end of the policy range of NZ$0.12 to NZ$0.125, up from NZ$0.11 in 2024, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The board also increased its dividend payout ratio in the range of 70% to 90% of normalised free cash flow, up from its previous policy of 60% to 70%, the filing said.

The company reaffirmed its 2025 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization guidance in the range of NZ$89 million to NZ$94 million, the filing added.

CHI also said it will introduce a dividend reinvestment plan and consider a dual Australian Stock Exchange listing.

Shares of the company rose past 3% in recent Friday trade, hitting their highest since February 2019.

data.title: Japan's super-long yields hover near record highs on inflation, fiscal concerns
data.description:
Japanese government bonds remained stable on Friday, after a volatile week in which super-long yields hit record highs as inflation and fiscal concerns sapped demand for debt.

Yields on 20-, 30-, and 40-year Japanese government bonds have been climbing, fuelled by growing concerns over the country's worsening fiscal health, as some political parties advocate for consumption tax cuts to counter rising prices.

Data on Friday showed Japan's core consumer inflation hit 3.5% in April, its fastest annual pace in more than two years, and keeping pressure on the Bank of Japan to continue raising interest rates.

A weak auction of 20-year bonds on Tuesday underscored the market's diminishing capacity to absorb new debt needed to finance the government's fiscal deficit.

BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda said on Thursday that the central bank will closely monitor market developments, as yields on super-long JGBs climbed to record highs this week.

"The risk of JGBs becoming 'indigestible' in the ultra-long term zone remains," Mizuho analysts said in a research note on Friday. "The incentive to reduce issuance of super-long bonds (thereby shortening the duration) is relatively strong."

The 30-year JGB yield (JP30YTN=JBTC) rose 1 basis point to 3.175%, hovering near an all-time high of 3.185% hit on Wednesday. The 40-year yield slid half a basis point to 3.665%, edging lower from the record 3.675% touched on Thursday.

The benchmark 10-year JGB yield (JP10YTN=JBTC) fell 0.5 basis point to 1.555%. The two-year yield (JP2YTN=JBTC) rose 0.5 basis point to 0.735%, and five-year yield (JP5YTN=JBTC) was flat at 1.03%.
